meta.description: This toy allele score resource demonstrates how allele-level scores can be stored in a tabix-indexed TSV file using 0-based coordinates. Each row represents a specific allele defined by chromosome, position, reference allele, and alternative allele, and provides both a numeric allele score and a categorical allele class.

Each row represents a specific allele defined by chromosome, position, reference allele, and alternative allele, and provides both a numeric allele score and a categorical allele class.

meta.description: This toy gene model resource demonstrates how transcript structures can be represented in GTF format. It represents the same minimal gene model as the toy RefSeq gene models resource, including one gene, one transcript, one exon, UTR, CDS, start codon, and stop codon features using 1-based GTF coordinates.

This toy gene model resource demonstrates how transcript structures can be represented in RefSeq/UCSC tabular format. It represents the same minimal gene model as the toy GTF resource, including one gene, one transcript, and one exon on the mini_genome, using 0-based half-open coordinates.

This toy gene score resource demonstrates how gene-level annotations can be stored in a gzip-compressed CSV file keyed by gene name. It includes a minimal example gene with supporting metadata columns and a numeric gene score.

scores.desc: Toy numeric gene score

meta.description: This toy gene set collection resource demonstrates how gene-set membership can be represented in GMT format. In GMT format, each row defines one gene set: the first column gives the gene set name, the second column gives a description, and the remaining columns list the genes assigned to that set. This resource represents the same toy gene-set membership as the map-format example: geneA is assigned to two toy gene sets, Function1 and Function2.
meta.summary: Toy GMT-format gene set collection resource

meta.description: This toy gene set collection resource demonstrates how gene-set membership can be represented using separate map and map-name files. The map file assigns each gene to one or more gene set identifiers, while the map-name file provides the display names for those identifiers. This resource represents the same toy gene-set membership as the GMT-format example: geneA is assigned to two toy gene sets, Function1 and Function2.
meta.summary: Toy map-format gene set collection resource

meta.description:
Nucleotide sequence of a toy genome containing two chromosomes, chr1 and chr2. This minimal genome is intended for testing and demonstration purposes. The chromosome sequences are shown below:

>chromosome1 TATGAAATAA

>chromosome2 AAAAAAAAAA

The accompanying dataprep.sh script runs samtools faidx on mini.genome.fa to create the FASTA index file used for random-access sequence queries.
Test annotation pipeline for mini-GRR

This toy position score resource demonstrates how per-position genomic scores can be stored in a bedGraph file and queried as a tabix-indexed resource. The file uses 0-based coordinates on the mini genome, with one score for each position on chr1 and chr2. The fourth column contains the numeric score assigned to each position.

The accompanying dataprep.sh script compresses mini\_positionscore\_bedgraph\_0.bedgraph with bgzip and creates a tabix index using tabix -p bed.

scores.desc: Toy numeric position score from a bedgraph file

meta.description:
This toy position score resource demonstrates how genomic scores can be stored in a bedGraph file where each row assigns one numeric score to a genomic region on the mini genome. The file uses 0-based half-open coordinates, where the start position is included and the end position is excluded. The fourth column contains the numeric score assigned to each region.

The accompanying dataprep.sh script compresses mini\_positionscore\_interval\_bedgraph\_0.bedgraph with bgzip and creates a tabix index using tabix -p bed.

scores.desc: Toy numeric position score from a bedgraph file with regions

meta.description:
This toy position score resource demonstrates how genomic scores can be stored in BigWig format. This resource contains the same toy position-score information as the accompanying interval bedGraph resource, `mini_positionscore_interval_bedgraph_0`, but stores the scores as a binary indexed BigWig file.

The accompanying ``dataprep.sh`` script first sorts ``mini_positionscore_bw_0.bedgraph`` by chromosome and start position, then uses ``bedGraphToBigWig`` together with ``chrom.sizes`` to create ``mini_positionscore_bw_0.bw``. Unlike tabix-based resources, the BigWig file is a binary indexed file and does not require a separate tabix index. 

Source bedGraph data: 
| Chromosome | Start | End | Score | 
|:----------:|:-----:|:---:|:-----:| 
| chr1 | 0 | 5 | 0.0 | 
| chr1 | 5 | 10 | 0.1 | 
| chr2 | 0 | 5 | 0.2 | 
| chr2 | 5 | 10 | 0.3 |

scores.desc: Toy numeric position score from a BigWig file

meta.description:
This toy position score resource demonstrates how per-position genomic scores can be stored in a tab-separated file and queried as a tabix-indexed resource. The file has no header and contains three columns: chromosome, 0-based position, and numeric score. The score applies to the specified position on the mini genome. This resource contains the same toy position-score information as the accompanying 1-based position score resource, ``mini_positionscore_tabix_1``, but stores positions using 0-based coordinates.

Data, shown in compact form:

| Position | chr1 | chr2 |
|:---------:|:-----------:|:-----------:|
| 0 | 0.0 | 0.0 |
| 1 | 0.1 | 0.1 |
| 2 | 0.2 | 0.2 |
| 3 | 0.3 | 0.3 |
| 4 | 0.4 | 0.4 |
| 5 | 0.5 | 0.5 |
| 6 | 0.6 | 0.6 |
| 7 | 0.7 | 0.7 |
| 8 | 0.8 | 0.8 |
| 9 | 0.9 | 0.9 |

The accompanying dataprep.sh script compresses mini\_positionscore\_tabix\_0.tsv with bgzip and creates a tabix index using the chromosome column and the position column.

scores.desc: Toy numeric 0-based position score from a tabix-indexed TSV file

meta.description:
This toy position score resource demonstrates how genomic scores can be stored in a tab-separated file with chromosome, start, end, and score columns, and queried as a tabix-indexed resource. This resource contains the same toy position-score information as the accompanying 1-based interval position score resource, ``mini_positionscore_tabix_interval_1``, but stores coordinates using 0-based positions.

The accompanying ``dataprep.sh`` script compresses ``mini_positionscore_interval_tabix_0.tsv`` with ``bgzip`` and creates a tabix index using the chromosome, start, and end columns.

meta.description:
This toy position score resource demonstrates how two per-position genomic scores can be stored in a single uncompressed tab-separated file. The file has no header and contains four columns: chromosome, 1-based position, numeric score, and categorical score. The numeric score is read as `pos_tsv_1`, and the categorical score is read as `pos_tsv_1_st`. Empty categorical values are treated as missing values.

This resource uses 1-based positions on the mini genome and extends the simpler TSV position-score examples by including both a numeric and a categorical score column.

Data, shown in compact form:

| Position | chr1 numeric | chr1 category | chr2 numeric | chr2 category |
|:--------:|:------------:|:-------------:|:------------:|:-------------:|
| 1 | 0.0 | good | 0.0 | good |
| 2 | 0.1 | good | 0.1 | bad |
| 3 | 0.2 | good | 0.2 | bad |
| 4 | — | — | 0.3 | bad |
| 5 | 0.4 | good | 0.4 | bad |
| 6 | 0.5 | good | 0.5 | bad |
| 7 | 0.6 | — | 0.6 | bad |
| 8 | 0.7 | bad | 0.7 | bad |
| 9 | — | bad | 0.8 | bad |
| 10 | 0.9 | good | 0.9 | bad |
meta.summary: 1-based tsv position score resource with two scores

scores.desc: Toy 1-based numeric position score from an uncompressed TSV file

meta.description:
This toy allele score resource demonstrates how allele-level scores can be stored in a VCF file and queried as a bgzip-compressed, tabix-indexed resource. The VCF contains three toy variants on the mini genome and defines three INFO fields: an integer score, a floating-point score, and a string score. Since the resource configuration does not list scores individually, GAIn extracts all the available allele scores from the VCF INFO field definitions.

The accompanying ``dataprep.sh`` script extracts the VCF header into ``mini_vcf.header.vcf``, compresses both ``mini_vcf.vcf`` and ``mini_vcf.header.vcf`` with ``bgzip``, and creates tabix indexes for both compressed files using ``tabix -p vcf``.

This toy allele score resource demonstrates how allele-level scores can be stored in a VCF file and queried as a bgzip-compressed, tabix-indexed resource. The VCF contains three toy variants on the mini genome and defines three INFO fields: an integer score, a floating-point score, and a string score. This resource is similar to ``mini_vcf_all``, but the VCF records intentionally omit one INFO value in each variant record. It demonstrates how GAIn handles allele scores that are defined in the VCF header but missing for some individual alleles.

The accompanying ``dataprep.sh`` script extracts the VCF header into ``mini_vcf.header.vcf``, compresses both ``mini_vcf.vcf`` and ``mini_vcf.header.vcf`` with ``bgzip``, and creates tabix indexes for both compressed files using ``tabix -p vcf``.

This toy allele score resource demonstrates how selected allele-level scores can be stored in a VCF file and queried as a bgzip-compressed, tabix-indexed resource. The VCF contains six toy variants on the mini genome and defines INFO fields for integer, floating-point, and string scores. Unlike mini_vcf_all, this resource lists scores explicitly in the resource configuration rather than extracting all INFO fields automatically.

This resource is designed to demonstrate custom plotting for a categorical allele score. The string INFO field is configured as a categorical score, and its histogram uses the custom plotting function customplot.py:plot_clnsig. This allows the resource to test how GAIn handles user-defined histogram plots in addition to standard VCF-based allele score annotation.

The accompanying ``dataprep.sh`` script extracts the VCF header into ``mini_vcf.header.vcf``, compresses both ``mini_vcf.vcf`` and ``mini_vcf.header.vcf`` with ``bgzip``, and creates tabix indexes for both compressed files using ``tabix -p vcf``.

scores.desc: description
description

meta.description:
This toy allele score resource demonstrates how a subset of allele-level scores can be selected from a VCF file and queried as a bgzip-compressed, tabix-indexed resource. The VCF contains three toy variants on the mini genome and defines three INFO fields: an integer score, a floating-point score, and a string score.

Unlike ``mini_vcf_all``, this resource specifies only two of the available INFO fields in the resource configuration: ``vcf_1_int`` and ``vcf_1_str``. Therefore, GAIn extracts only these two allele scores from the VCF, while the floating-point INFO field is ignored by this resource.

scores.desc: description
description
